questions.question: What is the primary acquisition channel for high-touch service businesses?

options.text: Cold direct-response advertising
High-volume cold emailing
Referral network cultivation and relationship-based sales
Posting memes on social media

questions.explanation: Service businesses rely on high trust. Referrals and existing relationships have the highest trust, making them the most effective channel for landing high-value consulting or agency deals.

questions.question: Why is 'Cold Outreach' generally harder for services than for SaaS products?

options.text: Service buyers don't use email
The price and commitment are significantly higher, requiring a higher 'Trust Threshold'
Service providers are bad at writing emails
It isn't harder; it works the same for both

questions.explanation: A $20,000 project requires much more trust than a $20/month app. Approaching someone cold for a high-stakes service often feels premature unless you focus on diagnosis and relationship-building first.

questions.question: In a service business context, what is a 'Referral Node'?

options.text: A piece of software that automates referrals
A specific person who is well-connected and consistently sends you business
A networking event
A technical term for a sales lead

questions.explanation: Referral Nodes are the 'power connectors' in your network—past clients, non-competing peers, or industry influencers who can introduce you to many qualified prospects.

questions.question: Which set of courses should a Service Business founder prioritize first (Tier 1)?

options.text: Courses 4, 8, and 14
Courses 5, 10, and 13
Courses 14, 18, and 11
Courses 7, 20, and 22

questions.explanation: For services, the priorities are Course 14 (Discovery), Course 18 (Proposals/Pricing), and Course 11 (Social Proof/Referrals).

questions.question: What is the recommended approach for 'Selective Outreach' in a service business?

options.text: Mass email 500 LinkedIn leads a day
Focus on 'Diagnosis' and highly researched, personalized value-adds for a small group of dream clients
Wait for them to find you
Buy a lead list of CEOs

questions.explanation: Because trust is paramount, selective outreach means doing the homework. Showing a prospect you already understand their specific problem is the fastest way to earn the right to a deeper conversation.

questions.question: Identify your top 3 potential referral partners (people who serve your ICP but don't compete) and one 'Dream Client.' Explain why these partners are a good fit for you and what specific value-add or diagnostic insight you could share with the dream client to open a conversation.
questions.promptForAI: Check for 'Referral Logic' — why do these partners serve the same people? The value-add for the dream client must be specific and based on observation, not just 'I can help you.'
